content.full_text:
La Sierra, California, United States.
15, May 1, 1991; Wednesday. 17:00 pacific time.

California Fair Political Practices Commission Staff,

13, May 1, 1991; Monday Our United States Postal Service delivered Political Reform Act of 1974 as Amended to January 1, 1991 - California Government Code Sections 81,000 through 91,015 in perfect condition.

I thank you all very very very much for dispatching this law to me because I am notas frequently at Riverside County Law Library as I have been in the passed and I am considering forming possibly two (2) uncontrolled committees both in support of candidates.

If you all want to send me more I could use a 490; 420; a list of currently declared candidates that is those who have filed 501's including candidates for U.S. President.

I wonder if Mr. Tsongas declared here in California to be a candidate for U.S. President ???

The most significant question I have about starting an uncontrolled committee is can I start an uncontrolled committee for a candidate who has yet to declare his candidacy via a 501 ??? And then what are the consequences of say starting an uncontrolled committee then the candidate never declares ??? This is why I have this question.

If you can refer me to any particular CAL.F.P.P.C. Bulletins for helpful information please do.

Any particular considerations if these uncontrolled committees are for a U.S. Congressional (House of Representatives) candidate (DEM) and a U.S. Presidential Candidate seeking candidacy under the "Independent Candidates" provisions of Our California Elections Code ???

I am, and reply to,
Mr. Charles Anthony F. Szychowski.

The image contains a reversed and partially cut-off text. Here is the readable part:

PARTICIPATE IN GOVERNMENT.

CS:cs, bothsides of most of a page.
thank you!!!
reply if you want to.

Paid for by Charles A. Szycowski Committee, 840,330,
4,215 Moby Avenue,
La Sierra,
California,
United States,
9,25,05-17,49.
